design_philosophy.core_loop: Select anthropomorphic animal fighter, learn and customize special move sets via password system, compete in tournament bracket against progressively challenging opponents to eventually face Dali Llama for the Belt of Heaven championship
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastering customizable combat movesets while adapting to opponent patterns; balancing learned attacks against execution skill
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Becoming the ultimate martial artist by discovering and perfecting unique fighting techniques, culminating in championship victory
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Innovative move-learning system where players acquire and save new attacks via passwords rather than traditional progression—prioritizes player agency in combat customization over linear character development. Commits to anthropomorphic animal cast with distinct martial arts archetypes (kung-fu bunny monk, Soviet bear commando, Thai boxer rat) rather than human fighters. View-blocking backgrounds are intentional design choice, suggesting artistic vision prioritized over functional clarity

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through move-learning system—players can acquire and customize special attacks to match their skill level and playstyle, effectively tuning encounter difficulty. Password system enables experimentation with different move combinations before committing to tournament run

progression.upgrade_system: Move-learning system allowing acquisition of new special attacks that can be saved via password codes. Players accumulate and customize their fighter's moveset between matches, creating personalized fighting styles
progression.unlock_mechanism: Secret button sequences unlock hidden playable characters (Karate Croc, Dali Llama). Brutal Unleashed expands roster with Chung Poe and Psycho Kitty. Tournament structure gates advancement through match victories
replayability_hooks: Multiple distinct fighters with unique martial arts archetypes encourage different playstyles
Move-learning system enables completely different movesets on replays via password experimentation
Two-player versus mode for competitive engagement
Brutal Unleashed sequel adds new characters and arenas, extending content

world_building_techniques: Character archetypes convey world through profession/identity (rock star lion, politician vixen, ex-Soviet commando bear, cult member panther)
Tournament framing device (Brutal Island Tournament held every four years) establishes competitive martial arts world
Character relationships implied through descriptions (vendetta between Prince Leon and Tai Cheetah/Kendo Coyote; mentorship dynamic between Tai Cheetah and Kendo Coyote)
Cinematic presentation praised for quality (noted as 'among the best seen on Sega CD')
Sequel narrative: Brutal Unleashed re-contextualizes tournament as Dali Llama's test of 'warrior's spirit' four years later

boss_design.philosophy: Final opponent Dali Llama serves as ultimate skill gate and narrative climax—defeating the tournament grants opportunity to challenge the reigning Belt of Heaven champion. Boss appears to scale in difficulty relative to player progression through tournament bracket
notable_bosses: Dali Llama - llama, current Belt of Heaven champion, framed as world's greatest fighter, tournament objective

faction_indicators: The Pantha - member of sinister cult (ideological affiliation/danger signal)
Ivan the Bear - ex-Soviet commando (geopolitical/military identity)
Foxy Roxy - politician and social activist (institutional power/ideology)
Prince Leon of Kenya - rock star (cultural authority)
Tai Cheetah/Kendo Coyote dynamic - mentorship with estrangement (loyalty/betrayal faction narrative)
danger_signals: The Pantha's cult membership suggests unorthodox/dangerous ideology
Kendo Coyote's motivation by greed implies moral flexibility
Ivan's claim to be strongest and Soviet military background suggests authoritarianism
Prince Leon's undisclosed vendetta indicates hidden conflict
environmental_storytelling: Uncharted Brutal Island as neutral tournament ground—suggests world outside civilization where only martial prowess determines status
View-blocking backgrounds (intentional design per reviews)—create atmospheric depth but obscure clarity, mirroring tournament's hidden dangers
Multiple arena environments across ports suggest diverse martial arts traditions and cultural spaces
